title: Rich Savory Mexican Chicken Tacos
description: A lazy Sunday Mexican dish featuring shio-cure and mirin-hon flavors, this Whole30-friendly recipe is perfect for a relaxing afternoon meal. It combines salty and fluffy textures with the richness of Mexican cuisine.

ingredients.name: taco seasoning
ingredients.notes: make sure it is Whole30 compliant

instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together mirin, hon dashi, and shio cure to make the marinade.
Add the chicken pieces to the marinade and mix well to coat.
Let the chicken marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Heat the coconut oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Remove the chicken from the marinade and cook in the skillet until browned on all sides.
Transfer the chicken to a baking dish and bake in the oven for 20-25 minutes or until cooked through.
While the chicken is cooking, prepare the taco seasoning according to the packet instructions.
Once the chicken is done, shred it with two forks and mix with the taco seasoning and lime juice.
Warm the lettuce leaves by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Assemble the tacos by spooning the chicken mixture onto a lettuce leaf and topping with diced tomatoes and avocado slices.
